name: Potential Registry or Environment Variable Unload
notes: This rule triggers when changes are made to environment variables or registry. This could indicate a precursor to a ransomware attack.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition:  Hide Schedule Task Via Index Value Tamper
BB:BehaviorDefinition: ETW Logging Tamper In .NET Processes
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Write Protect For Storage Disabled
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Hide Schedule Task Via Index Value Tamper, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Write Protect For Storage Disabled, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Sysmon Driver Unload, BB:BehaviorDefinition: ETW Logging Tamper In .NET Processes
TD_list: APPLY Potential Registry or Environment Variable Unload on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition:  Hide Schedule Task Via Index Value Tamper, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Write Protect For Storage Disabled, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Sysmon Driver Unload, BB:BehaviorDefinition: ETW Logging Tamper In .NET Processes
RED_list: Event name: Potential Registry or Environment Variable Unload
Event description: Detects when changes are made to environment variables or registry. This could indicate a precursor to a ransomware attack.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
